per_question.expected_preview: The bail! macro in Zed provides early error returns. Found in lmstudio.rs, vim/command.rs, agent_ui
per_question.answer_preview: bail! macro returns Err with error message, control flow exits immediately. Differs from Err(anyhow!("msg")) by being a return statement (not expression). Behavior: bail!("msg") -> return Err(anyhow!(

per_question.expected_preview: In crates/client/src/telemetry.rs (lines 82-90), Zed implements event integrity verification using Z
per_question.answer_preview: ZED_CLIENT_CHECKSUM_SEED for event validation: security checksum prevents fake telemetry events. Computed in client, sent with event. Server verifies checksum before accepting. Seed value hashed into

per_question.expected_preview: BackgroundExecutor in GPUI is the runtime for async tasks. When cx.spawn() is called, it creates a T
per_question.answer_preview: BackgroundExecutor schedules async tasks. cx.spawn() calls get scheduled on BackgroundExecutor via task queue. Executor is single-threaded WASM executor or thread pool executor depending on platform.

per_question.expected_preview: In crates/settings/src/keymap_file.rs (lines 69-75), KeymapSection has a use_key_equivalents boolean
per_question.answer_preview: use_key_equivalents field: when true, keymap respects non-QWERTY keyboard layouts. Maps key codes to actual characters typed. Purpose: support non-English keyboards. Platform support: macOS and Linux

per_question.expected_preview: util::defer() (seen in peer.rs line 156) implements the defer pattern - run code when scope exits re
per_question.answer_preview: util::defer() implements RAII cleanup: runs closure when scope exits (even on panic). Relationship: defer() is RAII pattern without explicit Drop. Used in peer.rs connection handling to clean up resou

per_question.expected_preview: The buffer will panic with an assertion failure. The code explicitly asserts that transaction_depth
per_question.answer_preview: Calling Buffer::end_transaction() when transaction_depth is 0: panics with assertion failure (debug) or undefined behavior (release). Constraint: must only call end_transaction() after calling begin_t

per_question.expected_preview: All three operations require that transaction_depth equals 0. The code enforces this with `assert_eq
per_question.answer_preview: push_transaction, pop_undo, pop_redo constraint: transaction_depth must be 0 (not in transaction). These operations modify undo/redo stacks which can't be modified mid-transaction. Calling during tran

per_question.expected_preview: The has_conflict flag is set to true (line 1629 in buffer.rs). When this flag is true, the buffer is
per_question.answer_preview: Buffer reload with concurrent edits: file_is_unsynced flag set when conflict detected (edits in buffer + disk changes). Flag prevents auto-saving, prompts user. Constraint: unsaved edits preserved unt

per_question.expected_preview: The invariant is that start <= end always (selections are stored in canonical order). The set_head m
per_question.answer_preview: Selection invariant: start and end represent range, start <= end always. set_head() maintains this: if new head < start, updates end to new head, swaps; if new head > end, extends end. Preserves invar

per_question.expected_preview: An edit is considered undone if its undo_count is odd (modulo 2 equals 1), as seen in line 69 of und
per_question.answer_preview: UndoMap: edit is considered 'undone' if it exists in undo stack (removed from redo stack). Determined by checking which stack contains the edit.

per_question.expected_preview: The operation is deferred and stored in the deferred_ops OperationQueue. The can_apply_op method (li
per_question.answer_preview: Remote operation arriving but buffer can't resolve anchors yet: operation deferred in deferred_ops queue. When buffer stabilizes, deferred ops replayed. Anchors resolve to new offsets after replay.

per_question.expected_preview: Merged operations have their timestamps stored in the merged_operations vector (line 1377 in buffer.
per_question.answer_preview: Buffer::merge_into_base for branch buffers: operations merged into base version. Tracked in merged_operation_ids to prevent double-application. Important for collaborative editing: ensures operations

per_question.expected_preview: always_deny takes absolute precedence over always_allow. According to the precedence documentation i
per_question.answer_preview: Tool permission precedence: always_deny takes precedence over always_allow. If both patterns match command, deny wins. Security-first approach.

per_question.expected_preview: If any regex patterns in a tool's rules failed to compile (stored in invalid_patterns), the tool is
per_question.answer_preview: Invalid regex patterns in tool permissions: parsing fails, throws error. Pattern marked as invalid, treated as non-matching. Security feature: prevents buggy regex from accidentally allowing/denying t

per_question.expected_preview: It does NOT override always_deny patterns. Even with always_allow_tool_actions=true, if an always_de
per_question.answer_preview: always_allow_tool_actions global setting does NOT override tool permissions when: (1) specific always_deny pattern matches, (2) extension-specific permissions restrict. Global setting is last resort,

per_question.expected_preview: The was_dirty_before_starting_transaction field is set only on the outermost transaction (when trans
per_question.answer_preview: Nested transaction constraint: was_dirty_before_starting_transaction flag only updated at outermost transaction level. Inner transaction nesting doesn't reset this. Ensures accurate dirty tracking acr

per_question.expected_preview: The disjoint field contains non-overlapping selections (line 31-32 in selections_collection.rs), whi
per_question.answer_preview: SelectionsCollection: disjoint selections guaranteed non-overlapping. pending selections are tentative selections being edited. Invariant: active selection in disjoint set, pending selections not yet

per_question.expected_preview: Fragment IDs must be strictly increasing (line 1773 in text.rs): `assert!(fragment.id > prev_fragmen
per_question.answer_preview: BufferSnapshot::check_invariants enforces: all fragment IDs unique and sequential. Critical because: coordinates computed from fragment IDs, duplicates break offset calculations.

per_question.expected_preview: The diff is adjusted to account for intervening edits, and conflicting parts are discarded (line 227
per_question.answer_preview: Buffer::apply_diff with old version: operation fails or applies incorrectly. Constraint: diff must be based on current buffer version. Old diff may reference deleted lines or invalid offsets. System d

per_question.expected_preview: When capability is Capability::ReadOnly (defined at line 87 in buffer.rs), the buffer cannot be edit
per_question.answer_preview: ReadOnly capability constraint: all edit operations fail when buffer capability is ReadOnly. Prevents any insertion/deletion. Affects normal editing and programmatic edits equally.

per_question.expected_preview: The operation is ignored/rejected. Lines 3007-3011 in buffer.rs check if the stored selection set ha
per_question.answer_preview: UpdateSelections with older lamport_timestamp: ignored/rejected. Preserves causal ordering. Operations with older timestamp treated as stale.

per_question.expected_preview: push_empty_transaction creates a parent transaction without clearing the redo stack (lines 2448-2460
per_question.answer_preview: push_empty_transaction: adds empty transaction to undo history. Purpose: create bookmark in undo history at significant moments. Caller responsibility: ensure no edits applied while transaction open,

per_question.expected_preview: The group_interval field (default 300ms for production, 0 for tests per lines 196-200 in text.rs) de
per_question.answer_preview: History transaction grouping constraint: transactions grouped only within specific time window (e.g., 300ms). After timeout, new transaction starts separate group. Grouping_interval enforced in Histor

per_question.expected_preview: The buffer validates that the anchor's buffer_id matches the snapshot's remote_id (lines 2306-2316 i
per_question.answer_preview: Before resolving Anchor: system checks that Anchor belongs to current buffer version. If version mismatch: fails with error. Critical check prevents resolving stale anchors to wrong positions.

per_question.expected_preview: The saved_version is updated in did_save (line 1529) and reload operations (line 1620-1621). When up
per_question.answer_preview: saved_version updated only when Buffer::save() completes successfully. At same time, unsaved_changes flag cleared. Constraint: saved_version and unsaved_changes kept synchronized.
